defs: To decorate with fringe or embroidery.
An embroidered and puckered border; a hem or fringe, often of gold or silver twist; also, a pleat or fold, as of a band.
quotes: A triumphant chariot made of carnation velvet, enriched withpurl and pearl.

defs: An inversion of stitches in knitting, which gives to the work a ribbed or waved appearance.

records.ety: [Cf. Sw. porla, and E. pur to murmur as a cat.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Purled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Purling.]

defs: To run swiftly round, as a small stream flowing among stones or other obstructions; to eddy; also, to make a murmuring sound, as water does in running over or through obstructions.
quotes: Swift o'er the rolling pebbles, down the hills, Louder and louder purl the falling rills.

defs: To rise in circles, ripples, or undulations; to curl; to mantle.
quotes: thin winding breath which purled up to the sky.

defs: A circle made by the notion of a fluid; an eddy; a ripple.
quotes: Whose stream an easy breath doth seem to blow, Which on the sparkling gravel runs in purles, As though the waves had been of silver curls.

defs: A gentle murmur, as that produced by the running of a liquid among obstructions; as, the purl of a brook.

Malt liquor, medicated or spiced; formerly, ale or beer in which wormwood or other bitter herbs had been infused, and which was regarded as tonic; at present, hot beer mixed with gin, sugar, and spices.
